ip_intent.one_line_desc: Interfaces and manages integrated non-volatile RRAM memory; supports scrambling, integrity, secure wipe and emulated OTP access.
ip_intent.one_paragraph_desc:
RRAM Controller interfaces the integrated, non-volatile RRAM memory with software and other hardware components in the system, such as Life Cycle Controller, Key Manager, and OTP Controller.
It consists of the open-source RRAM controller that interfaces with a third party RRAM macro.
The protocol controller handles direct memory mapped read requests, software controlled read and write requests, as well as life cycle RMA entry.
The controller supports a differentiation between info and data partitions, RRAM memory protection at page boundaries, and the handling of key manager secrets inaccessible to software.
The data partition of the RRAM is further divided into a data region and an OTP region.
The controller handles OTP read and write requests that are emulated with a read-set-write algorithm on the OTP region.
The actual physical controller is highly parameterized (number of pages, number of words and word size for each page, and number of read buffers) and supports XEX scrambling configurable by software, as well as ECC support configurable on a page boundary.

param_list.name: RndCnstAddrKey
param_list.desc: Compile-time random bits for default address key

param_list.name: RndCnstDataKey
param_list.desc: Compile-time random bits for default data key

param_list.name: RndCnstAllSeeds
param_list.desc: Compile-time random bits for default seeds

param_list.name: RndCnstLfsrSeed
param_list.desc: Compile-time random bits for initial LFSR seed

param_list.name: RndCnstLfsrPerm
param_list.desc: Compile-time random permutation for LFSR output

param_list.name: ExecEn
param_list.desc: Constant value that enables RRAM execution

param_list.name: SecScrambleEn
param_list.desc: Compile-time option to enable RRAM scrambling

alerts.name: fatal_err
alerts.desc:
RRAM fatal errors including uncorrectable ECC errors.

Note that this alert is not always fatal.
The underlying error bits in the !!FAULT_STATUS register remain set until reset, meaning the alert keeps firing.
This doesn't hold for !!FAULT_STATUS.PHY_RELBL_ERR.
To enable firmware dealing with multi-bit ECC errors during firmware selection and verification, these error bits can be cleared.
After passing this stage, it is recommended that firmware classifies the corresponding alert as fatal on the receiver end, i.e, inside the alert handler.

alerts.name: fatal_macro_err
alerts.desc: Fatal errors triggered inside the RRAM macro, including fatal TL-UL bus integrity faults of the test interface.

alerts.name: recov_macro_err
alerts.desc: Recoverable errors triggered inside the RRAM macro.

rtl/rram_ctrl.sv.doc_comment:
Copyright lowRISC contributors (OpenTitan project).
Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0, see LICENSE for details.
SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0

RRAM Controller Module:
The RRAM controller manages access to the vendor macro or the open-source emulation model.
The controller can be accessed through two main interfaces:
- host_tl (read-only memory mapped access to data partition of the RRAM)
- core_tl (to configure the controller and to issue read/write transactions to the RRAM)
The controller has two additional hardware plugs:
- rram_ctrl_lcmgr: This FSM reads seeds and scrambling keys from the RRAM and handles RMA-Req.
- rram_ctrl_otp: otp_ctrl can issue init, read, write and zeroize commands to the OTP partition
    of the RRAM.
The controller supports memory protection and scrambling.
